prose: Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS, density-fit RI on by default), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied nitrobenzene.xyz as-is (no re-optimization at this level). Mulliken partial charges (PySCF) were used for finite-difference Fukui index construction; the N±1 states used charge ±1, multiplicity 2 (doublet). The headline result is the maximum f⁺ value (electrophilic attack index): 0.49641781, located at atom 4 (C, ipso carbon, 1-based), meaning C1 — the carbon directly bonded to the nitro group — is the most electrophilically reactive site. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is atom 5 (C, ortho carbon) with f⁻ = 0.10728820. The dual descriptor is maximally positive at atom 4 (Δf = +0.51070984) and maximally negative at atom 3 (N, nitro nitrogen, Δf = −0.47394433). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. Fukui charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported by the engine. CLI invocation: cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard nitrobenzene.xyz. Output plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-182835_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
